title: Hard-skipped/mocked SDK suites hid a broken compatibility matrix — always smoke against a live server before release
content: Release validation for 1.3.0 ran each SDK against a live release server for the first time and found: C# HTTP reads all silently returning defaults (envelope never unwrapped + two wrong command names), C# native transports throwing on HDEL/HINCRBY (dead mapper keys), Python queues broken on every transport (payload not byte-encoded), hash.incrby incrementing by 1 silently (payload key mismatch), server missing HINCRBY on both native dispatchers and the whole stream family on RESP3, and transactional writes silently bypassing MULTI on native transports. None of this was caught because C# S2S tests are hard-skipped ([Fact(Skip=...)]), Python S2S is env-gated and never ran in CI, and unit tests mock at the layer above the bug. Lessons: (1) a per-SDK 3-transport smoke harness against a real server is mandatory pre-release; (2) hard-skip attributes on S2S tests rot — gate on an env var instead so they can actually run; (3) response-shaping code needs tests at the transport boundary, not just mocked module tests; (4) when the same dotted command map exists in 3 SDKs, diff the op-name lists against the server dispatch table mechanically (comm -23) — it found kv.delete/hash.values instantly.
